segments.text: In Kubernetes, every object can have labels — key-value pairs you attach to Pods, Services, Deployments, and more.
Labels are how you organize your cluster.
Environment: production. App: nginx. Tier: backend.
They're not just decoration — selectors use labels to target resources.
There are two kinds of selector.
Equality-based: app equals nginx. App not equals frontend.
And set-based: app in web, api. Or: environment not in staging, dev.
A Service uses a label selector to decide which Pods receive its traffic.
A Deployment uses selectors to know which Pods it owns.
One rule: no logical OR. Multiple requirements use AND — all must match.
Labels identify. Selectors connect. That's the wiring behind everything in Kubernetes.
